title: Pilot teaching layer: exercises + solutions + notebook for the cohort
context: A dataset without a teaching layer (exercises, solutions, notebook) is not a complete teaching artifact. The pilot teaching layer pairs with the synthetic survival-analysis cohort from pilot-008 to create a complete lesson: defined learning objectives, graded exercises, worked solutions, and a runnable Jupyter notebook guiding learners through fitting and interpreting a Kaplan-Meier curve and Cox model. The teaching layer must be reviewed by an educator reviewer (pedagogy check) and a technical reviewer (notebook runs, solutions correct). The safety notice must appear in the notebook's first cell.
objective: Produce the teaching layer for the M0 pilot cohort: defined learning objectives, >= 1 graded exercise with a worked solution, and a runnable Python notebook, reviewed by an educator and a technical reviewer.
acceptanceCriteria: Learning objectives clearly stated (e.g. 'fit and interpret a Kaplan-Meier curve; fit a Cox proportional-hazards model with a censored outcome; interpret hazard ratios').
At least one graded exercise with a complete worked solution; exercises use only the synthetic cohort data (no external data required).
Runnable Python Jupyter notebook: first cell contains the mandatory safety notice; notebook runs top-to-bottom without errors in a standard conda/pip environment; includes instructor notes.
Educator reviewer has reviewed and confirmed the teaching layer actually teaches the stated skill at the stated level.
Technical reviewer has confirmed the notebook runs and solutions are correct.
Document committed to the project repo alongside the pilot-008 dataset; commit DCO signed-off.
resources: open-teaching-datasets-pilot-008 (the cohort data file this lesson uses)
open-teaching-datasets-template-001 (exercises{} schema and safety notice text)
output: A teaching-layer document set (exercises, solutions, runnable Jupyter notebook, instructor note) committed alongside the pilot-008 cohort dataset, completing the M0 pilot as a full teaching artifact.
